title: 10 best herbal teas for headaches
intro_desc: Herbal teas for headaches are caffeine-free, warm beverages made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices. They are a natural and effective way to alleviate headaches, as they contain soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. Some popular herbal teas for headaches include chamomile, ginger, feverfew, and willow bark. Chamomile tea, for instance, helps relax muscles and reduce stress, which can trigger headaches. Ginger tea, on the other hand, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease headache pain. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can find relief from headaches and improve their overall quality of life. For example, a stressed-out student who frequently suffers from tension headaches can drink chamomile tea before bed to relax and reduce the likelihood of headaches. Similarly, a construction worker who experiences migraines due to inflammation can consume ginger tea during breaks to alleviate pain and continue with their workday comfortably.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cohosh tea helps with headaches because it contains phytoestrogens, which are plant-based compounds that can mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. Fluctuations in estrogen levels are known to trigger headaches and migraines in some individuals, particularly women. By regulating estrogen levels, black cohosh tea can help alleviate headache symptoms. Additionally, black cohosh has anti-inflammatory properties, which can further reduce headache pain and inflammation in the blood vessels surrounding the brain. Drinking black cohosh tea may provide natural relief for those who experience headaches related to hormonal changes or inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Black cohosh tea contains salicylic acid, which helps reduce inflammation, a common cause of headaches.
Analgesic: The isoferulic acid and triterpene glycosides in black cohosh have pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate headache pain.
Antispasmodic: Black cohosh tea can help relax muscles and alleviate tension, which may contribute to the onset of headaches.
Nervine: The nervine properties of black cohosh tea help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger headaches.
Vasodilatory: Black cohosh tea contains compounds that can help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the risk of headaches caused by vasoconstriction.
remedy_parts: Roots: Black cohosh tea made from the roots is commonly used to treat headaches due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of black cohosh contain active compounds that help alleviate headaches, especially those related to menstrual cycles and hormonal imbalances.
Leaves: The leaves of black cohosh are used less frequently than the roots and rhizomes, but they can still provide some relief for headaches due to their soothing and calming effects.
Flowers: Although not as potent as the roots or rhizomes, black cohosh flowers can be used in tea to help reduce headache symptoms, thanks to their mild anti-inflammatory properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo tea helps with headaches because it contains alkaloids, particularly sanquanxie, which has been found to effectively inhibit the contraction of blood vessels in the brain. This action helps to improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and relieve pressure in the head, thereby alleviating headache symptoms. Additionally, yanhusuo tea has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its calming and sedative properties, which can help to reduce tension and anxiety, further contributing to headache relief.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: Yanhusuo tea contains alkaloids such as tetrahydropalmatine, which have pain-relieving properties, helping to alleviate headaches.
Anti-inflammatory: The tea contains compounds like ligustilide and ferulic acid, which reduce inflammation, contributing to headache relief by soothing irritated blood vessels in the head.
Vasodilatory: Yanhusuo tea helps relax and widen blood vessels, thanks to its active constituents like senkyunolide I and senkyunolide H, improving blood flow and reducing headache symptoms.
Sedative: The tea contains compounds like tetrahydropalmatine and cnidilide, which have calming and relaxing effects, aiding in headache relief by reducing tension and anxiety.

Antioxidant: Yanhusuo tea is rich in antioxidants like ferulic acid and ligustilide, which protect cells from damage, supporting overall brain health and potentially reducing the frequency and severity of headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yanhusuo tea's roots contain alkaloids that help reduce the frequency and severity of headaches.
Rhizomes: Yanhusuo tea's rhizomes have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate headache symptoms.
Leaves: Yanhusuo tea's leaves contain compounds that help relax blood vessels, reducing pressure and pain in the head.
Flowers: Yanhusuo tea's flowers have calming effects that can help reduce tension and stress-related headaches.
Seeds: Yanhusuo tea's seeds contain essential oils that can help soothe and relax the mind, reducing headache symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew tea helps with headaches because it contains parthenolide, a compound that is believed to inhibit the release of prostaglandins, chemicals that cause inflammation and pain in blood vessels surrounding the brain. Additionally, feverfew may also reduce the sensitivity of nerve endings in the head, further alleviating the symptoms of headaches. Furthermore, feverfew tea has been shown to have muscle relaxant properties, which can help relieve tension headaches caused by muscle contractions in the head and neck. Drinking feverfew tea regularly may also help prevent the onset of headaches by maintaining the body's natural balance and reducing inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Feverfew tea's ability to reduce inflammation is important for headaches, particularly migraines, as they are often caused by inflammation in the blood vessels of the brain, and are attributed to parthenolide, a key active compound in feverfew.

Vasoactive properties: Feverfew tea helps regulate the dilation and constriction of blood vessels, which is crucial for headache relief, and can be attributed to parthenolide and other sesquiterpene lactones.

Analgesic properties: Feverfew tea's pain-relieving qualities are essential for alleviating headache discomfort, and are due to the presence of parthenolide and other active compounds, such as flavonoids and volatile oils.

Serotonin modulation: Feverfew tea can help regulate serotonin levels, which is important for headache relief since serotonin plays a role in the narrowing of blood vessels leading to headaches; this is linked to parthenolide's ability to inhibit serotonin release.

Antioxidant properties: Feverfew tea's antioxidant effects help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to headaches; these antioxidant properties are attributed to various compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the feverfew plant are commonly used in medicinal tea due to their ability to help relieve headaches and migraines.
Flowers: The flowers of the feverfew plant are used in tea to help alleviate symptoms of headaches, thanks to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Feverfew seeds are sometimes included in medicinal tea for headaches, as they contain parthenolide, a compound believed to help reduce the frequency and severity of headaches.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tea helps with headaches because it contains naturally occurring compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. One of the key active components in maidenhair tree is ginkgolide A, which has been shown to inhibit platelet-activating factor (PAF), a substance that can cause inflammation and pain. Additionally, maidenhair tree tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Drinking maidenhair tree tea may help improve circulation, reduce muscle tension, and alleviate headaches caused by stress or tension. Its soothing and calming effects may also help promote relaxation and reduce the frequency and severity of headaches.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair tree tea helps reduce inflammation, which is important for headaches as inflammation can cause or worsen headache symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Analgesic: Maidenhair tree tea has pain-relieving properties, which is crucial for headaches as it helps alleviate the pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and volatile oils.

Vasodilatory: Maidenhair tree tea can help widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the tension that can cause headaches. The active constituents responsible for this property include prostaglandins and nitric oxide.

Anxiolytic: Maidenhair tree tea has calming and anxiety-reducing effects, which is important for headaches as stress and anxiety can trigger or worsen headache symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: Maidenhair tree tea has antioxidant properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and damage that can contribute to headaches. The active constituents responsible for this property include polyphenols and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and are known to have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in alleviating headaches.
Buds: Rich in essential oils and antioxidants, they can help relax muscles and reduce tension, providing relief from headaches.
Stems: Known for their calming effects, stems can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for headaches.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids that have been shown to have a positive impact on blood flow, helping to alleviate headaches caused by constriction of blood vessels.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with headaches because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These properties can help to reduce the swelling and inflammation that can cause headaches, as well as to alleviate the pain associated with them. Additionally, licorice tea can help to balance hormones and reduce stress, both of which can contribute to the development of headaches. The soothing and calming effects of the tea can also help to promote relaxation and reduce tension, which can further help to alleviate headaches.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can alleviate headaches.
Analgesic: The analgesic effects of licorice tea, attributed to glycyrrhizin and certain flavonoids, aid in relieving pain associated with headaches.
Antispasmodic: Licorice tea's antispasmodic properties, resulting from glycyrrhizin and various isoflavonoids, help relax smooth muscles, reducing muscle tension that contributes to headaches.

Adaptogenic: The adaptogenic properties of licorice tea, owing to glycyrrhizin and other active constituents, help regulate cortisol levels and support the body's response to stress, which can help prevent stress-induced headaches.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of licorice tea, attributed to flavonoids and other polyphenols, combat oxidative stress and protect nerve cells from damage, reducing the risk of developing headaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate headaches.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to help reduce the severity of headaches.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice tea plant contain various essential oils, which can help relax muscles and alleviate tension headaches.
Bark: The bark of the licorice tea plant contains isoliquiritigenin, which has been shown to have pain-relieving effects and may help reduce the frequency of headaches.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with headaches because it contains properties that can reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels, which can help alleviate the pain associated with headaches. The tannins found in witch hazel have astringent properties that can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby reducing the pressure that can cause headaches. Additionally, witch hazel contains antioxidants and flavonoids that can help to relax the blood vessels and improve circulation, which can further help to reduce the frequency and severity of headaches. Drinking witch hazel tea can provide a natural and effective way to manage headaches and promote overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea helps reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can alleviate headaches caused by inflammation or tension. This is due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids.

Vasoconstrictor: Witch hazel tea helps narrow blood vessels, reducing pressure and pain in the head, which is particularly helpful for headaches caused by dilated blood vessels. The active constituent responsible for this is gallic acid.
Analgesic: Witch hazel tea has pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate headache symptoms. This is attributed to the presence of several compounds, including tannins and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which can help protect the brain and nervous system from damage and reduce oxidative stress, a potential cause of headaches. The primary antioxidants in witch hazel are flavonoids and proanthocyanidins.

Sedative: Witch hazel tea has mild sedative properties, which can help relax muscles and promote sleep, alleviating tension headaches. This is due to the presence of volatile oils, such as eugenol and hexenol.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel tea plant contains high levels of tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from headaches.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are rich in antioxidants and volatile oils, which can help alleviate tension and ease headache symptoms.
Twigs: The twigs of the witch hazel tea plant are often used in teas for headaches due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant contain fragrant oils that can help relax blood vessels and provide relief from headaches caused by tension or stress.
Seeds: The seeds of the witch hazel tea plant are a good source of fatty acids and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and soothe headache pain.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the plant contain active compounds that can help reduce swelling and provide relief from headaches caused by sinus pressure or congestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's Wort tea helps with headaches because it contains several key compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds work to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which can help to alleviate the tension and pressure that often cause headaches. Additionally, St. John's Wort is believed to have a positive effect on neurotransmitter levels in the brain, particularly serotonin and dopamine, which can help to regulate mood and reduce the perception of pain. Drinking St. John's Wort tea may also help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can further help to prevent headaches from occurring.
remedy_properties: Nervine relaxant: St. John's wort tea has a relaxing effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate headaches caused by tension or stress. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Anti-inflammatory: The tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which is a common cause of headaches. Flavonoids and tannins present in the plant contribute to its anti-inflammatory effects.
Analgesic: St. John's wort tea has pain-relieving qualities that can help manage headache discomfort. Hypericin and hyperforin are believed to be responsible for its analgesic effects.

Antidepressant: The tea can help balance mood and alleviate depression, which is often linked to chronic headaches. Hypericin, hyperforin, and other components like hypericins and flavonoids contribute to its antidepressant properties.

Anxiolytic: St. John's wort tea can reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which may help prevent headaches triggered by anxiety or stress. Hypericin and hyperforin have been found to have anxiolytic effects, contributing to the tea's headache-relieving properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with headaches because it contains certain compounds, such as linalyl acetate and linalool, which have been shown to have relaxing and analgesic properties. These compounds may help to reduce muscle tension and inflammation, which are common causes of headaches. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of lavender can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to headaches. Drinking lavender tea may also help to promote better sleep, which can be beneficial for those who experience headaches due to lack of sleep or poor sleep quality. Overall, the combination of these effects makes English lavender tea a natural and effective remedy for headaches.
remedy_properties: Analgesic: English lavender tea contains compounds like linalyl acetate and linalool, which have pain-relieving properties that can help alleviate headaches.
Anti-inflammatory: The tea's active constituents, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, reduce inflammation, helping to soothe headaches caused by inflamed blood vessels.
Anxiolytic: English lavender tea has calming effects due to its ability to modulate GABA receptors, reducing anxiety and tension that can contribute to headaches.
Sedative: The tea's sedative properties, attributed to its essential oils and polyphenols, can promote better sleep and relaxation, helping to prevent headaches caused by sleep disturbances.
Antispasmodic: English lavender tea contains compounds that help relax smooth muscles, reducing muscle tension and spasms that can lead to headaches.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in relaxing essential oils, which can help alleviate tension and reduce headache symptoms.
Leaves: Contain high levels of soothing antioxidants, which can help decrease inflammation and ease headache pain.
Buds: Packed with calming compounds, promoting relaxation and reducing the frequency and intensity of headaches.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort tea helps with headaches because it contains natural compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce tension and alleviate headaches. The tea is also known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels surrounding the brain and thus help to relieve headache pain. Additionally, motherwort tea is a natural sedative, which can help to promote relaxation and reduce the stress and anxiety that can often trigger headaches. Drinking motherwort tea can also help to improve blood circulation, which can further help to alleviate headache symptoms. Overall, motherwort tea is a natural and effective remedy for headaches, providing relief without the need for harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Nervine: Motherwort tea has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate tension-type headaches caused by stress and anxiety. The active constituents responsible for this property are alkaloids, such as stachydrine and caffeic acid.
Anti-inflammatory: Motherwort tea helps reduce inflammation, which can contribute to headaches. This property is attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides, such as aucubin and catalpol.
Analgesic: Motherwort tea has pain-relieving properties, which can help manage headache discomfort. The flavonoids in motherwort, such as luteolin and hispidulin, contribute to this effect.

Vasodilatory: Motherwort tea helps widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the risk of headaches caused by vasoconstriction. The iridoid glycosides and flavonoids in motherwort are responsible for this property.

Anxiolytic: Motherwort tea can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help prevent headaches triggered by stress and anxiety. The alkaloids and flavonoids in motherwort contribute to this anxiolytic effect.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the motherwort tea plant are commonly used for making medicinal tea for headaches due to their calming and relaxing properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the motherwort tea plant are used in medicinal tea for headaches because they contain active compounds that help reduce tension and anxiety.
Seeds: The seeds of the motherwort tea plant are used in medicinal tea for headaches due to their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Flax tea helps with headaches because it contains omega-3 fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the body, including the brain. Additionally, flax tea is a good source of magnesium, a mineral that can help relax blood vessels and reduce tension, which is a common cause of headaches. Furthermore, flax tea is caffeine-free, making it a great alternative to traditional caffeinated beverages that can sometimes trigger headaches. Drinking flax tea regularly may help prevent headaches by keeping the body hydrated and providing it with essential nutrients.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Flax tea can help reduce inflammation, which is often a contributing factor in headaches, thanks to the presence of omega-3 fatty acids.
Analgesic: Flax tea has natural pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate headache symptoms, due to the presence of compounds like linamarin and linustatin.
Antioxidant: Flax tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the body from damage and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to headaches, thanks to the presence of lignans.
Relaxant: Flax tea has calming and relaxing properties, which can help reduce muscle tension and ease headaches, due to the presence of magnesium and other calming compounds.
Mood-enhancing: Flax tea can help improve mood and reduce anxiety, which can help alleviate headaches, due to the presence of tryptophan and other mood-enhancing compounds.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Flax seeds are rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate headaches.
Leaves: Flax leaves contain beneficial compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to help relieve pain and reduce the frequency of headaches.
Stems: Flax stems are a good source of dietary fiber, which can help regulate digestion and reduce tension, thereby alleviating headaches.
Flowers: Flax flowers contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from headaches.

Roots: Flax roots have been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including headaches, due to their high levels of mucilage, which can help soothe and protect the digestive tract.
